mission.title: argo-events: Parameterization a block of k8s resource (set of key values)
mission.description: Parameterization a block of k8s resource (set of key values). This issue affects 17+ users.

steps.title: Identify argo-events troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your argo-events deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n argo-events -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo-events
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo-events -n argo-events --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review argo-events configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant argo-events configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n argo-events -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo-events
kubectl get configmap -n argo-events -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=argo-events
```
I am trying to parametrize an entire block of k8s trigger using payload. the payload converted to string with backslashes and can't be injected to overwrite the block.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Parameterization a block of k8s resource (set of key values)
steps.description:
Apply the configuration change to resolve the issue:
```yaml
Actual state: "{\"image\":\"docker/whalesay:latest\",\"command\":[\"cowsay\"],\"args\":[\"injected\"]}"
 Desired state: "{"image":"docker/whalesay:latest","command":["cowsay"],"args":["injected"]}"
```

steps.title: Confirm Parameterization a block of k8s resource (set of… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo-events -n argo-events --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n argo-events --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary: See the source issue for the community-verified solution: https://github.com/argoproj/argo-events/issues/2272

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with argo-events installed or the issue environment reproducible.
